2604.06737.title: Luwen Technical Report
2604.06737.paper_summary: Large language models have demonstrated remarkable capabilities across a wide range of natural language processing tasks, yet their application in the legal domain remains challenging due to the specialized terminology, complex reasoning requirements, and rapidly evolving legal knowledge involved. In this paper, we present Luwen, an open-source Chinese legal language model built upon the Baichuan foundation model through three key techniques: continual pre-training on a large-scale legal corpus, supervised fine-tuning with carefully curated legal instruction data, and retrieval-augmented generation integrated with a comprehensive legal knowledge base. We evaluate Luwen on five representative legal tasks spanning both prediction and generation settings, including legal judgment prediction, judicial examination, legal text summarization, law article question answering, and judicial decision reasoning. Experimental results show that Luwen outperforms several strong baselines, demonstrating the effectiveness of our approach in adapting general-purpose language models to the legal domain.
2604.06737.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型在各種自然語言處理任務中展現了卓越的能力，但由於涉及專業術語、複雜的推理要求以及快速變化的法律知識，其在法律領域的應用仍然具有挑戰性。<lb/>在本文中，我們介紹了 Luwen，一個基於 Baichuan 基礎模型的開源中文法律語言模型，通過三個關鍵技術構建而成：在大規模法律語料庫上進行持續的預訓練、使用精心策劃的法律指導數據進行監督微調，以及與全面的法律知識庫集成的檢索增強生成。<lb/>我們在五個代表性的法律任務上評估 Luwen，這些任務涵蓋了預測和生成設置，包括法律判決預測、司法考試、法律文本摘要、法律條文問答和司法決策推理。<lb/>實驗結果顯示，Luwen 超越了幾個強基準，證明了我們的方法在將通用語言模型適應於法律領域方面的有效性。
2604.06737.author: Yiquan Wu et.al.
2604.06737.authors: Yiquan Wu, Yuhang Liu, Yifei Liu, Ang Li, Siying Zhou, Kun Kuang
